user_query:
I’m a retired vet who helps organize our friendly community debate nights (I back the other candidate, but we keep it civil). I have a quick-and-dirty Python script that pulls event details from a saved HTML page, and I want to share a cleaned-up version with the group.

Please do a focused code review and refactor of scripts/collect_events.py using input/events.html, then produce cleaned outputs and a short email for the club:

1) Inspect the existing script and HTML so you understand what’s being extracted. Then produce a refactored script at refactored/collect_events_refactored.py that, when run from the workspace root with no arguments, reads input/events.html and writes both:
   - output/events.json (UTF-8, a JSON array of objects), and
   - output/events.csv (UTF-8, with a header row).
   The extracted fields for each <div class="event"> must be: id (from data-id), title (text inside h3.title), date_iso (normalized as YYYY-MM-DD from the time[datetime] attribute), city, state, topics (list of li strings under ul.topics), and host. Sort the records by date_iso ascending in both files. In the CSV, store topics as a single semicolon-separated string in a "topics" column. If a field is missing, include the record and use an empty string for that field.

2) Make the refactoring improvements clear and maintainable: avoid hard-coded paths in internals (default to input/events.html and output/ when no args are provided), separate parsing and I/O into functions, and ensure the JSON is valid (not Python repr). Keep dependencies to the standard library only.

3) Draft a concise email (3–6 sentences) addressed to “Debate Night Tech Team” saved to output/email_to_club.txt. In natural prose, describe what you improved in the script, how to run it from the workspace root, and what files it generates. Include two quick concrete benefits (e.g., deterministic output, parameterized paths). Also include the number of events parsed and the list of distinct cities (comma-separated) derived from output/events.json so folks can cross-check.

Don’t modify files under input/. Leave the original script in place for comparison. The success criteria are: refactored/collect_events_refactored.py exists and runs locally without extra installs; output/events.json and output/events.csv exist with the specified fields and sorted order; and output/email_to_club.txt contains the counts and cities computed from the generated JSON. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
